title: thymus gadorensis
intro: Thymus gadorensis, also known as Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Thymus gadorensis include reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Thymus gadorensis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to promote wound healing, soothe sore throats, and calm the mind.
